answer:
For COPD patients with frequent exacerbations, the preferred inhaler therapy is LABA+LAMA combination therapy. Inhaled corticosteroids are added for patients with blood eosinophils >=300 cells/uL or continued exacerbations.

[Source: GOLD COPD Report, 2024]

arguments.query: preferred inhaler therapy for COPD patients with frequent exacerbations

result.query: preferred inhaler therapy for COPD patients with frequent exacerbations

results.content: For COPD patients with frequent exacerbations, LABA+LAMA combination therapy is preferred. Inhaled corticosteroids are added for patients with blood eosinophils >=300 cells/uL or continued exacerbations.
results.citation: COPD Diagnosis, Management and Prevention, GOLD, 2024

results.content: Oral anticoagulation is recommended for patients with atrial fibrillation and a CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more in men or 3 or more in women. Direct oral anticoagulants are preferred over warfarin in most eligible patients.
results.citation: Anticoagulation in Atrial Fibrillation, ACC/AHA/HRS, 2024

results.content: Urate-lowering therapy is recommended for patients with recurrent gout flares, tophi, or radiographic damage, with allopurinol as the preferred first-line agent, titrated to a serum urate target below 6 mg/dL.
results.source: ACR Guideline for Management of Gout
results.citation: Management of Gout, ACR, 2020

results.content: For moderate to severe depression, a combination of antidepressant medication and high-intensity psychological therapy such as cognitive behavioral therapy is recommended over either treatment alone.
results.source: NICE Guideline on Depression in Adults
results.citation: Management of Depression in Adults, NICE, 2022

results.content: Low-dose aspirin starting at 12-28 weeks of gestation is recommended for patients at high risk of preeclampsia. Magnesium sulfate is recommended for seizure prophylaxis in patients with severe features of preeclampsia.
results.citation: Gestational Hypertension and Preeclampsia, ACOG, 2020

results.content: High-intensity statin therapy is recommended for patients with clinical atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and for primary prevention in adults 40-75 years with LDL-C >=190 mg/dL or diabetes with additional risk factors.
results.citation: Management of Blood Cholesterol, ACC/AHA, 2018

results.content: Intravenous alteplase is recommended within 4.5 hours of symptom onset for eligible patients with acute ischemic stroke. Mechanical thrombectomy is recommended for large-vessel occlusion within 24 hours in selected patients.
results.citation: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke, AHA/ASA, 2021

metadata.title: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke

results.content: For adults and adolescents with asthma, as-needed low-dose ICS-formoterol is preferred as reliever therapy over short-acting beta-agonist alone, even at the mildest step of treatment, to reduce exacerbation risk.
results.citation: Global Strategy for Asthma Management, GINA, 2024

results.content: In patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF), guideline-directed medical therapy includes ARNI/ACEi/ARB, beta-blockers, mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists, and SGLT2 inhibitors. SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended regardless of diabetes status.
results.citation: Management of Heart Failure, AHA/ACC/HFSA, 2022

results.content: In patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, an SGLT2 inhibitor is recommended for those with eGFR >=20 mL/min/1.73m2 to reduce CKD progression and cardiovascular risk. ACE inhibitors or ARBs are first-line for patients with albuminuria and hypertension.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Chronic Kidney Disease in Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

result.disclaimer: Evidence excerpts for professional review; not a diagnosis.
